payload.evidence_summary: Stored DB text and strong external first-party/store evidence consistently describe a first-person single-player horror campaign built around investigation, pendulum-based environmental interaction, puzzle solving, and stealthy avoidance/escape from supernatural threats instead of direct combat. The structure is authored and story-driven across cursed island locations, which fits survival_horror better than action_horror or psychological_horror. The only noted discrepancy is whether the pendulum has 4 or 5 mechanics, which does not materially affect the gameplay identity.

research_payload.evidence_summary: Authoritative first-party/store sources are detailed and internally consistent on the game's core identity: a first-person, single-player narrative horror game centered on investigation, puzzle-solving, pendulum-based environmental interaction, and stealth-based avoidance of supernatural threats rather than direct combat. A minor discrepancy exists on the number of pendulum mechanics (official site says 5; major store pages say 4), but it does not materially change the gameplay profile. Deluxe content appears non-gameplay only.

core_loop: Investigate Alan Rebels' father's disappearance by exploring the cursed island of Godstone and uncovering occult clues.
Use the mystical pendulum to interact with and modify the environment, expose clues, and manipulate occult objects.
Solve puzzles and perform paranormal/detective-style investigation to keep progressing.
Survive hostile supernatural encounters by avoiding, hiding from, or escaping entities rather than fighting them head-on.
combat: Official sources explicitly state Alan avoids direct combat.
Threat management is framed around stealth, hiding, sneaking, pursuit, and escape.
No weapon-based combat, offensive powerset, or direct combat loop is described in the consulted sources.
progression: Progression is primarily narrative/investigative: unravel what happened on Godstone and advance through the story.
Progress is gated by pendulum-driven environmental interaction plus puzzle solving.
A Daedalic press release says the demo covers the game's opening chapter, supporting story/chapter progression.
No explicit RPG leveling, loot chase, or skill-tree progression is described in the consulted sources.
Source discrepancy: the official website says the pendulum has 5 mechanics, while Steam/PlayStation store pages say 4.
structure: Single-player story campaign set across Godstone locations such as foggy streets, buildings, interiors, and wilderness areas.
Exploration is tense and atmosphere-led, with supernatural encounters interrupting investigation.
Available evidence points to guided narrative progression through authored locations rather than a systems-driven sandbox or multiplayer structure.
modes: Single-player only in the consulted official/store sources.
Offline play is listed on PlayStation; no co-op, competitive, or other multiplayer modes were identified.
dlc_relationship: The identified Deluxe/Upgrade DLC is non-gameplay bonus content.
Deluxe content consists of the complete soundtrack and a digital artbook with over 200 pages of behind-the-scenes material.
No gameplay-expanding DLC, extra story chapter, or combat/content expansion was identified in the consulted sources.

source_notes: Steam store page is the strongest single gameplay source: it specifies first-person play, survival-horror framing, pendulum-based environmental interaction, stealth/no direct combat, puzzles, paranormal detective work, and single-player packaging.
The official game website corroborates first-person horror/investigation and stealth-based avoidance, but lists 5 pendulum mechanics instead of the 4 listed on store pages.
The PlayStation store page independently confirms 1 player and repeats the feature set, strengthening confidence in the first-party gameplay description.

Daedalic's January 12, 2026 press release adds concrete demo-level gameplay detail: opening chapter structure, clue exposure with the pendulum, investigation through wilderness/haunted spaces, and a supernatural chase/escape sequence.
The Steam Deluxe Upgrade Pack page confirms the currently identified DLC is bonus soundtrack/artbook material only, not gameplay content.

payload.evidence_summary: Stored and external official evidence consistently identify Machine Child as a single-player daughter-raising / nurturing simulation centered on choices, skills, bonds, rumors, lessons, leisure, shopping, Tarot/Inspiration effects, 700+ story events, and 22 branching endings. The structure is replayable, authored branching life-sim progression with relationship growth and no described dedicated combat layer, which strongly fits life_sim over RPG or adventure labels.

research_payload.evidence_summary: Strong official evidence identifies Machine Child as a single-player daughter-raising / nurturing simulation. Steam, the JAST store page, and the official website consistently describe a loop of selecting one of three daughters and shaping her future through skills, bonds/connections, town rumors, lessons and leisure choices, shopping and outfit unlocks, seasonal trips, and Tarot/Inspiration systems that feed into 700+ story events and 22 endings. Official materials do not present a separate action or tactics combat layer; the gameplay is described instead as choice-, event-, and management-driven. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))

core_loop: Choose one of three daughters, then raise her over a long-form sequence of story events by making decisions about skills, bonds/connections, rumors, shopping, lessons, leisure, and trips, with those choices determining her future. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))

The game is built around accumulating and using systems such as Skills, Bonds/Connections, and Inspiration/Tarot effects to unlock events and steer toward different destinies. ([jastusa.com](https://jastusa.com/games/jast065/machine-child-pre-order))
combat: No dedicated combat system is described in the official feature lists; the cited materials frame play around management choices, events, rumors, lessons, shopping, affection, vacations, and Tarot outcomes rather than battle mechanics. This is an inference from the official descriptions. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))
progression: Progression is driven by the daughter's acquired skills and growing bonds/connections, which affect event access and ultimately which ending she reaches. ([jastusa.com](https://jastusa.com/games/jast065/machine-child-pre-order))

Outfits are unlockable through boutiques/shops and story events, and affection changes the daughter's home-screen voice responses. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))

Players collect Inspiration to draw Tarot cards with unique effects, and the game features 22 Tarot- or Arcana-linked endings. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))
Seasonal vacation content unlocks commemorative CG illustrations, with five seasonal/special scenes per daughter. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))
structure: The game is a branching, replay-oriented life/nurturing sim with 700+ story events and 22 unique endings. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))

Official materials describe event categories including little adventures, noble parties, encounters with otherworldly beings, random events, lessons, leisure activities, and town-rumor-triggered events. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))
There are three selectable daughters with distinct personalities: Froline/Florine, Cordelia, and Chole/Chloe. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))
modes: Steam lists the game as single-player. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))
dlc_relationship: A free 'Machine Child - Full Content DLC' exists on JAST as an optional restoration patch for the Steam version; JAST says it is not needed if the game was downloaded from the JAST Store. ([jaststore.com](https://jaststore.com/games/jast065_dlc/machine-child-full-content-dlc))

source_notes: Steam store page supplied release/platform/mode data plus the most concrete English gameplay description: 700+ events, 22 endings, outfits, affection responses, seasonal trips, and Tarot/Inspiration. ([store.steampowered.com](https://store.steampowered.com/app/3548040/Machine_Child/))

JAST Store page materially strengthened the gameplay evidence by naming the systems directly: Skills, Bonds, town rumors, Item Shop, Random Events, Lessons, Leisure Activities, and Arcana Paths. ([jastusa.com](https://jastusa.com/games/jast065/machine-child-pre-order))

The official Japanese website corroborated the daughter-raising sim identity and added story/setup language plus the Skills and Connections terminology and the 700+ events / 22 destinies structure. ([machine-child.com](https://machine-child.com/))

The JAST DLC page clarified the DLC relationship as an optional restoration patch for the Steam build rather than separate gameplay content. ([jaststore.com](https://jaststore.com/games/jast065_dlc/machine-child-full-content-dlc))
